title: Kevin Wants to Know

description.panel1: A person named Kevin asks God what the weather will be like today.
description.panel2: God responds that it will be very sunny.
description.panel3: God assures Kevin that there will be definitely no floods or anything coming.
description.panel4: Kevin sees an ark with animals boarding it and nonchalantly says "Cool."

emotion: The author satirizes those who take action without understanding the actual situation and making reasonable plans.

title: Dating Life

description.panel1: Two friends are sitting at a café; one asks the other about her dating life.
description.panel2: The second friend mentions she met a sexy vampire recently.
description.panel3: She continues to say that vampires are actually pretty good in bed.
description.panel4: She then adds that it gets weird when she is on her period.

emotion: The author humorously highlights the unconventional and awkward aspects of dating someone with unique characteristics, emphasizing that we should understand and respect everyone's different lifestyles.

title: Change of Mind

description.panel1: A frog advises a turtle, "Don't be afraid of change."
description.panel2: The turtle responds by asking, "What if the change is bad?"
description.panel3: The frog, looking startled and thoughtful, says, "I didn't consider that..."
description.panel4: The frog, now appearing scared, exclaims, "Change is scary as hell!!"

emotion: The author expresses the view that although people know the importance of change, they are still afraid of change.

title: but I ...

description.panel1: Two characters labeled "PAL" discuss something they dislike, with a third character looking annoyed.
description.panel2: The second "PAL" agrees with the first, and the third character continues to look displeased.
description.panel3: The third character wants to leave as soon as possible, and then the first two characters notice her.
description.panel4: The second characters labeled "PAL" say awkwardly "O-OH, But it is ok if it is you.".

emotion: The author humorously describes the embarrassment of some people not liking certain behaviors, but their friends happen to have those same behaviors.

title: A Cute Face

description.panel1: One bird tells another that it cannot understand how it can eat grasshoppers.
description.panel2: The bird continue talk that "I cannot eat anything with a cute face", the other bird says "BUT ..."
description.panel3: The second bird imagines a grasshopper with an exaggerated cute face.
description.panel4: The second bird gasps in shock at the imagined cute face of the grasshopper.

emotion: By describing the grasshopper's reaction to the conversation between the two birds, the author expresses that people should learn to be flexible in life.

title: 1:30 am

description.panel1: The character is sitting at a desk, looking at a computer screen, and the time is shown as 1:30 am.
description.panel2: The character hears a loud "BANG" and thinks it might have been one of the dogs.
description.panel3: The character turns to look back where two dogs are seen sleeping peacefully.
description.panel4: The character looks distressed and wonders, "Am I going to die?" after hearing another "BANG" sound.

emotion: The author conveys the unsettling and fearful atmosphere experienced late at night when unexpected noises lead to anxiety and fear.

title: Damnit Susan

description.panel1: A bird is chewing its cud and feeding a small bird on the table.
description.panel2: A person tries to interrupt them and says "Excuse me!", but the bird continues to feed.
description.panel3: The person comes to the table and tells the three birds that they must leave.
description.panel4: One of the two birds says to the other: "Damn it, Susan. I can't take you anywhere."

emotion: The author humorously describes the phenomenon that people will be politely expelled for improper behavior in public, and warns people to pay attention to their own image in public and not do bad things.

title: Unsung.

description.panel1: The pink character seems unimpressed as the blue character enthusiastically sings "Just a small town girl living in a lonely world!" while driving.
description.panel2: The pink character looks concerned and thinks to themselves, "He's going to sing the entire road trip..." as the blue character continues singing "Just a city girl born and raised..."
description.panel3: The pink character appears increasingly stressed as the blue character encourages them to sing along with "Don't stop! Believing! C'mon, sing along! Hold on to that fa..."
description.panel4: The car is shown flying off a cliff while the pink character falls out, and the blue character continues to sing "Street lights! People!"

emotion: By describing the inner thoughts and experiences of the pink character, the author expresses his emphasis on traffic safety and satirizes those drivers who ignore the safety of their passengers.

title: Single Step

description.panel1: A character is seen stepping out of a house saying "okay," with the caption "EVERY JOURNEY BEGINS WITH A SINGLE STEP."
description.panel2: The character takes a step away from the house with the encouraging caption "THERE YOU GO."
description.panel3: The character abruptly twists their foot with an expression of shock and pain.
description.panel4: The character tumbles to the ground in pain, exclaiming "OH SHIT... AAHHHH!!"

emotion: The author humorously portrays the idea that even simple beginnings can sometimes be unexpectedly challenging or painful, highlighting the unpredictability of starting new endeavors.

title: This is the Life

description.panel1: The bird looks to the right while humming "Check right".
description.panel2: The bird continued to hum "TAKE A BITE".
description.panel3: The bird looks to the left while humming "Check left".
description.panel4: The bird humming "Stay not dead".

emotion: The author expresses his love for life through this humming bird.

title: Memory Tripped.

description.panel1: Two characters are standing together, each holding a bottle and smiling.
description.panel2: The pink character suddenly reacts strongly, exclaiming "OH FUCK!" and drops their bottle.
description.panel3: The purple character asks "WHAT'S WRONG?!", while the pink character appears distressed and unable to speak.
description.panel4: The pink character, clinging to the purple character, explains that "I REMEMBERED HOW SHITTY MY LIFE IS".

emotion: The author highlights the sudden and overwhelming nature of negative realizations and how personal distress can disrupt seemingly happy moments, emphasizing the importance of social support during tough times.

title: Kick Me.

description.panel1: A green character informs a pink character that there is a "Kick Me" sign on his back.
description.panel2: The pink character responds that he is aware of the sign and that he put it there himself.
description.panel3: The pink character urges the green character to proceed with kicking them.
description.panel4: The pink character expresses a desire for his body to be punished.

emotion: By describing the dialogue between the pink character and the green character, the author emphasizes that people should cherish their bodies instead of destroying them.

title: Wing Man.

description.panel1: A blue character is approached with the greeting "Hey there, handsome."
description.panel2: The blue character becomes nervously excited and responds with "Oh, uh... h-hello."
description.panel3: A woman clarifies that she was talking to the bird on the blue character's head.
description.panel4: The bird on the blue character's head remarks, "Better luck next time, chump."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the embarrassment that can arise from miscommunication.

title: Anxiety.

description.panel1: A green scientist proudly tells a small robot, "I’ve done it, Robo-Kun! You now have... Humanity!"
description.panel2: The robot, worried and clutching its chest, says, "Oh thank you! I... What is... this awful thing I’m feeling?"
description.panel3: The green scientist responds, "It might be anxiety," to which the robot, in distress, exclaims, "Oh God."
description.panel4: The robot, now more frantic and wavering, screams, "I don’t want to be human anymore! I change my mind! I change my mind!!"

emotion: Through the description of the robot's performance, the author shows the horror of human anxiety and expresses that we should adjust our mentality in life and not be overly anxious.

title: Millennial Grandpa.

description.panel1: A green character enthusiastically shows a trophy labeled "Existence Trophy" to their grandpa sitting on a red sofa.
description.panel2: The grandpa angrily knocks the trophy out of the green character's hand.
description.panel3: The grandpa berates the green character, expressing that such trophies make kids entitled brats.
description.panel4: The grandpa angrily reflects on how in his day, a person had to participate to earn a trophy.

emotion: The author expresses a critique of the practice of awarding participation or "existence" trophies, suggesting that they foster entitlement and reflect a cultural shift from past values where rewards were earned through active participation.

title: Dead Inside

description.panel1: Two coffins are shown, with one saying "I feel dead inside..."
description.panel2: The other coffin responds, saying "You need to open up."
description.panel3: The first coffin opens, and a dead body falls out with a "thump."
description.panel4: The now empty coffin looks happy and says "Much better."

emotion: This cartoon humorously illustrates that inner stress needs to be relieved immediately.

title: Bones to Pick.

description.panel1: A dog is digging a hole while another dog asks "Why are you burying your bone?".
description.panel2: The first dog pours milk near the buried bone.
description.panel3: A bone rapidly grows out of the ground like a plant.
description.panel4: The bone grows into a large tree full of bones, surprising the two dogs.

emotion: The author provides a humorous punchline by comparing burying bones to growing plants.

title: Lost.

description.panel1: A yellow character is asked by a red character in a car if they know how to get downtown.
description.panel2: The yellow character responds by saying he grew up downtown and have spent most of their life there.
description.panel3: The yellow character begins to give directions, starting by acknowledging their current location on Eighth Avenue.
description.panel4: The yellow character internally panics, admitting they have no idea how to get anywhere and questioning what they're doing.

emotion: The author humorously demonstrates the phenomenon that people may not be familiar with the place where they grew up.

title: Death

description.panel1: A bird expresses that "I have been thinking about death recently", and another bird says "OH ? ".
description.panel2: Another bird inquires if there have been any revelations from the thoughts about death.
description.panel3: The first bird admits thinking about death being overwhelming and not ideal.
description.panel4: The first bird decides that it will not "do" death.

emotion: Through the dialogue between two birds, the author expresses the fear of death and the yearning for life.

title: Root Cause

description.panel1: A squirrel suggests to a bird that they should invite Drew to the party.
description.panel2: The bird dismisses the idea, saying that Drew always has an excuse.
description.panel3: Drew, represented as a flower, responds that he would love to come but is rooted to the ground.
description.panel4: The bird reiterates to the squirrel that Drew can't come, reinforcing his earlier statement.

emotion: The author uses humor to express that everyone has their own characteristics, which we cannot ignore.

title: Grow Up

description.panel1: A blue character is holding a baby and asking "Is it me, or is our baby... growing?".
description.panel2: A red character responds affirmatively, stating that "Yeah, that's what babys do. They grow.".
description.panel3: The blue character questions "Will it ever stop growing ? ", and the red character answers, "Eventually."
description.panel4: The blue character looks sad and contemplative while holding the baby.

emotion: Through the conversation between the couple, the author expresses his anxiety about the baby's growing up and conveys a thought about life.

title: that's what happened

description.panel1: A character with glasses is telling a story to their friend, who responds with, "oh wow."
description.panel2: The friend, with a shocked expression, says they would have "curled up and died" if the same thing happened to them.
description.panel3: The character with glasses laughs heartily in response to their friend's dramatic statement.
description.panel4: The character with glasses abruptly stops laughing, looking down and saying "yeah..."

emotion: The author satirizes the behavior of always avoiding problems in a humorous way.

title: Hopped Up

description.panel1: A frog is sitting on a lily pad in a pond.
description.panel2: A person excitedly approaches the frog, exclaiming, "A FROGGY! SO COOL!"
description.panel3: The person reaches out their hand towards the frog.
description.panel4: The frog, still sitting on the lily pad, thinks to itself, "im cool."

emotion: The author creates a humorous contrast between the excitement of humans and the calm confidence of frogs, expressing a humorous feeling that makes readers smile.

title: Scary Cat

description.panel1: One bird is sitting on a wooden surface, and the other bird calls it: "Hey, Jules!"
description.panel2: The other bird makes a "HHSSS" sound.
description.panel3: The first bird seems startled, and the second bird asks: "Scary cat! What do you think?"
description.panel4: The second bird looks worried and asks: "Oh no, isn't it too scary?"

emotion: The author uses the reactions of these two birds to express the differences between people. What one person is very afraid of may be commonplace to another person.

title: I Wonder

description.panel1: A hand with an extended middle finger is depicted.
description.panel2: The extended middle finger wonders why it has no friends.
description.panel3: Another hand waves hello, but the middle finger responds rudely.
description.panel4: The middle finger realizes the reason for not having friends.

emotion: The comic satirizes the behavior of wanting to make friends but not being polite.

title: Ginger Lemon

description.panel1: A monstrous figure demands, "Feed me fresh souls!" from a young girl.
description.panel2: The girl responds, "I only have tea."
description.panel3: The girl specifies, "It's ginger lemon."
description.panel4: The monstrous figure reluctantly agrees, saying, "Tea is fine, too."

emotion: The author uses humor to playfully contrast the terrifying demand of a monster with the mundane and polite response of a child, showing a humorous and unexpected resolution.

title: dodgeball

description.panel1: A girl successfully dodges a dodgeball that is thrown at her.
description.panel2: The girl continues to dodge another incoming dodgeball.
description.panel3: Two spectators observe, with one commenting that "She actually good at this.", while the other disagrees, says 'That is not quite it.'.
description.panel4: The girl appears frightened and sweating, clutching the dodgeball.

emotion: The author uses a humorous reversal to express that some people can do something well through fear.

title: sunny day

description.panel1: A person at a desk comments on the sunny day outside the window.
description.panel2: The person continues to look at the sunny view outside.
description.panel3: The person pulls down the window blinds.
description.panel4: The person sits at the desk with the blinds fully closed, blocking out the sunlight.

emotion: The author describes the cartoon character enjoying a beautiful sunny day but blocking the sunlight outside the window with curtains, expressing the author's determination to better focus on the task indoors.

title: Pool Party

description.panel1: A person in a yellow raincoat approaches a drain where a clown is saying, "Join us, Jimmy. We all float down here!"
description.panel2: The person in the yellow raincoat screams "AAAAAAH!" and runs away.
description.panel3: Clowns at a pool party comment on the runner, with one saying "He ran away!"
description.panel4: One clown suggests, "Maybe he doesn’t like pool parties." Another clown responds, "Try balloons next time."

emotion: The author juxtaposes traditional horror scenes with comical and lighthearted twists to create humor,.

title: Tough Call

description.panel1: A green character is on the ground and asks a blue character "I am dying to call 911."; the blue character looks hesitant and asks, "Call?"
description.panel2: The blue character, sweating nervously, holds a phone and asks, "C-can I text them?", while the green character insists, "You have to call!"
description.panel3: The blue character looks extremely anxious, sweating profusely while holding the phone.
description.panel4: The blue character apologizes with "Sorry," and The dead green character consoles him: "It's okay. The phone calls are the worst."

emotion: By depicting the blue character's fear of calling 911, which leads to the green character's death, the author satirizes those who mess up for various reasons at important moments.

title: Grass

description.panel1: Geese are flying in formation, and one of them excitedly remarks about their first migration.
description.panel2: A goose starts to wonder about the type of grass they will find at their destination.
description.panel3: The goose speculates on various types of grass, listing options like bluegrass, Bermuda, and fescue.
description.panel4: The goose continues listing more specific types of grass, such as dichondra, floratam, zoysia, and fine fescue.

emotion: The author expresses his love of travel through a humorous description of a wild goose's overly detailed and somewhat trivial attention to different types of grass during its journey.

title: Big Time

description.panel1: The blue character angrily tells the green character that they have seriously messed up.
description.panel2: The green character calmly responds with a smile, saying "ha ha, yes I did".
description.panel3: The blue character, still angry, asks the green one "Are you going to do anything about it?", while the green character still laughs, saying "ha ha".
description.panel4: The green character responds confidently and unbothered, saying "I will not".

emotion: The author satirizes the procrastination behavior in life, where people always drag their feet and refuse to correct mistakes even if they have made them.

title: Wait It Out

description.panel1: A priest informs Mr. and Mrs. Lutz that their house harbors the gateway to hell.
description.panel2: Mr. and Mrs. Lutz react casually, asking the priest what they should expect.
description.panel3: The priest lists various supernatural phenomena such as flies, black goo, and blood seeping through wallpaper, but notes that nothing is life-threatening.
description.panel4: Mr. and Mrs. Lutz, seemingly undeterred, discuss whether they should move out or wait it out.

emotion: The author expresses a sense of humor by contrasting the seriousness of the situation with the couple's leisurely attitude, making the readers smile.

title: Remember ...

description.panel1: The image shows pine tree branches with needles and the text "Remember..." whisper the trees.
description.panel2: The image shows withered tree leaves and the text "there are many things out of your control...".
description.panel3: The image shows seed pods hanging from a tree branch and the text "That's OK".
description.panel4: The image shows a branch with flower buds and the text "Find the things that are".

emotion: The author expresses a message about acceptance of things one cannot change and encourages focusing on the aspects of life that one can influence.

title: Break Out

description.panel1: An egg is starting to crack with the caption, "DON'T BE AFRAID TO OPEN UP TO OTHERS".
description.panel2: A bird is emerging from a cracked egg with the caption, "BREAK OUT OF YOUR SHELL".
description.panel3: The bird stands next to another uncracked egg with the caption, "REVEAL YOUR INNER BEAUTY".
description.panel4: The second egg has been fully cracked open, revealing a yolk with a face saying "hey", while the bird watches.

emotion: The comic conveys the message of encouraging others to open up and express their true selves, emphasizing that everyone has inner beauty that is worth sharing.

title: unknown strength ?

description.panel1: The two characters greet each other warmly.
description.panel2: The characters high-five each other vigorously.
description.panel3: After the high-five, one character feels a sharp pain in his hand.
description.panel4: The painful character holds his hand, and the other character also looks painful.

emotion: The comic humorously describes a common phenomenon in life, making readers who have similar experiences smile.

title: camera shy

description.panel1: A person takes a photo of another individual who makes an awkward face, expressing frustration at the repeated awkward expression.
description.panel2: The person taking photos looks at multiple pictures featuring the awkward expression and expresses a determination to capture a better photo when the individual is not expecting it.
description.panel3: The photographer instructs the subject to look in a different direction, and as the subject turns, the flash goes off, capturing another photo.
description.panel4: The photographer is shocked to see that the subject still managed to make the awkward face even when taken by surprise.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the challenge of photographing someone who is naturally camera-shy and the unexpected outcomes that can arise despite careful planning.

title: shaded

description.panel1: A character wearing sunglasses stands next to PAL.
description.panel2: "PAL" asks how the sunglasses-wearing character can see with those shades and if they are prescription.
description.panel3: The character with the sunglasses begins to lift them off their face.
description.panel4: The character reveals another pair of glasses underneath, causing "PAL" to react in surprise.

emotion: Through this character wearing two pairs of glasses, the author expresses the importance of protecting eyesight.

title: Tempting Fate

description.panel1: An elderly character dressed as a wizard declines an invitation over the phone, stating he must remain in his quarters.
description.panel2: A pink character on the other end of the phone sarcastically guesses the reason is the "ancient prophecy".
description.panel3: The wizard dramatically confirms the reason with, "It has been foretold!!"
description.panel4: The wizard is seen comfortably tucked in bed, reading a book.

emotion: The author humorously describes how people use exaggerated and dramatic excuses to avoid social situations, expressing the author's desire to be free and alone most of the time.

title: Time

description.panel1: A trilobite lies on the ground 400 million years ago with a primitive landscape in the background.
description.panel2: The trilobite remains on the ground 200 million years ago surrounded by early plants.
description.panel3: The same trilobite fossil is on the ground in 2017, with a flower and a butterfly nearby.
description.panel4: The fossil remains in the same place 200 million years into the future, surrounded by new plants.

emotion: The author emphasizes the constancy and longevity of fossils over long periods of time, contrasting them with the ever-changing environment around them, expressing a sense of humor.

title: Pushy.

description.panel1: A blue character looks at a red button while a green character says, "Every time you push this button, your life shortens by one year..."
description.panel2: The green character continues, "...but! In exchange, you’ll get..." as the blue character reaches to push the button.
description.panel3: The blue character repeatedly presses the button with determination, causing the green character to sweat and react nervously.
description.panel4: The green character grabs hold of the blue character, saying "Dude" in an attempt to stop him from pressing the button further.

emotion: By depicting the characters in the comic acting without regard for consequences, the author satirizes how some people may act impulsively or obsessively without considering the negative consequences.

title: Weather forecast

description.panel1: The news anchor introduces the weather segment and asks Claire if it's cold enough for her.
description.panel2: Claire responds, forecasting a warm and wet front coming through.
description.panel3: The news anchor jokes about feeling warm and wet just from hearing the forecast.
description.panel4: The news anchor jokes further, saying he won’t last much longer, and Claire laughs.

emotion: The author satirizes those who like to make inappropriate jokes in formal occasions.

title: high-heels

description.panel1: A person admires a cute pair of high-heeled shoes but expresses uncertainty about wearing heels.
description.panel2: The person tries on the high heels and initially feels positive, thinking they can handle walking in them.
description.panel3: The person, while attempting to walk confidently, starts to fall.
description.panel4: The person has fallen, and an employee, concerned, asks if they are okay.

emotion: The author conveys the humorous struggle and potential difficulty of walking in high-heeled shoes, highlighting the contrast between appearance and practicality.

title: Cause for Alarm

description.panel1: A person tells their alarm clock that its only purpose is to wake them up, and the alarm clock agrees.
description.panel2: The next day, the alarm clock rings and tells the person to wake up.
description.panel3: The person angrily yells at the alarm clock, telling it to shut up because they are trying to sleep.
description.panel4: The alarm clock looks sad and confused, trying to say something but stops.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the contradictory and often irrational relationship people have with their alarm clocks; although people actively set their alarms to wake them up, they feel frustrated when the alarms actually do their job.

title: Perceive Things

description.panel1: Two birds are talking, and one asks "Do you thought about how we might perceive things completely differently?".
description.panel2: The second bird responds, asking "You mean how one bird's "green" could be another's "blue".?"
description.panel3: The first bird excitedly agrees and adds that everyone’s perception of reality is unique and may be incoherent to anyone else.
description.panel4: The second bird comments that it sounds wild, and the first bird agrees with satisfaction.

emotion: Through the conversation between the two birds, the author expresses that everyone’s aesthetic taste is different, and we should respect the aesthetic tastes of different people.

title: Say No More

description.panel1: The character observes a heated debate labeled "Online Discourse" with two figures representing conflicting opinions, A and B.
description.panel2: The character contemplates joining the debate, saying, "I should weigh in..."
description.panel3: The character reconsiders and thinks, "Actually... no. No, I shouldn't."
description.panel4: The character concludes with the thought, "Sometimes, ya gotta tell yourself to STFU," while nodding.

emotion: The author conveys the idea that in some situations, particularly online debates or conflicts, it's wise to refrain from adding to the discourse and to keep one's opinions to oneself.

title: Horn Dog.

description.panel1: A character in a vehicle sees a bumper sticker on the car in front of them that says, "HONK IF YOU'RE HORNY."
description.panel2: The character is confused to notice that his horn has been honked.
description.panel3: The character begins to sweat nervously as he notices a hand honking his horn multiple times.
description.panel4: The character's dog in the passenger seat honks repeatedly.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the hesitation and awkwardness that a person may feel when responding to another person's prompting, comically highlighting the social discomfort and anxiety that can ensue.

title: hip

description.panel1: A person enthusiastically asks a group if they have seen "THIS" and says it's really good.
description.panel2: The group responds unenthusiastically, with one saying they are not a fan and another asking what it is.
description.panel3: The one of the group asks if they have seen "THAT," expressing love for it, and the group responds enthusiastically.
description.panel4: The person sits alone in front of a TV displaying "THIS," thinking that at least I like it.

emotion: The author illustrates the feeling of one's enthusiasm not being shared by others and finding contentment in personal enjoyment despite a lack of external validation.

title: No Deal

description.panel1: A frog asks a turtle how to deal with issues beyond its control.
description.panel2: The turtle responds that worrying about them non-stop is the way.
description.panel3: The frog questions how that helps, and the turtle admits that it doesn't.
description.panel4: The frog ironically responds with enthusiasm, thanking the turtle for the advice.

emotion: The author uses this comic to humorously highlight the futility of incessant worrying about things beyond one's control.

title: Forget

description.panel1: A man questions his cat for not wanting the cheese he gave her.
description.panel2: The man decides to eat the cheese himself while extending his hand towards it.
description.panel3: The man eats the cheese.
description.panel4: The man realizes that he didn't actually give the cat any cheese.

emotion: The author’s love for everyday life is highlighted in a lighthearted way by depicting humorous scenes where men forget what they are doing.

title: glasses

description.panel1: A character wearing glasses joyfully raises his arms with excitement.
description.panel2: Another character, also wearing glasses, reciprocates the excitement by raising her arms.
description.panel3: The two characters embrace passionately, one of them yells "Glasses!", and the two bump into each other.
description.panel4: Both characters are embarrassed because their glasses are crooked.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the impact of wearing glasses on daily life by depicting two people wearing glasses hugging and their glasses bumping into each other.

title: Goose Boost

description.panel1: A blue character stands next to a goose that is honking "HONK! HONK!"
description.panel2: The blue character looks nervous as the goose continues to look at them.
description.panel3: The goose starts honking more aggressively with "HUNK! HUNK!" while the blue character looks more distressed.
description.panel4: The goose honks again with "HONK!" as the blue character looks surprised with a slight blush.

emotion: By describing a person's confusion and embarrassment at the hands of a seemingly aggressive goose, the author highlights the relatable human experience of feeling overwhelmed in unexpected social interactions.

title: onions

description.panel1: A character is thinking "Chopping onions can't be that bad" while preparing to chop an onion.
description.panel2: The character starts chopping the onion and thinks, "This isn't bad at all".
description.panel3: The character pauses and appears to be feeling something unexpected.
description.panel4: The character is shown crying from the onion vapors.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common experience of underestimating the effect of onion vapors while chopping onions.

title: Moonrise

description.panel1: The moon tells the sun that it has arrived, so the sun can leave now, and the sun responds with "OK."
description.panel2: The sun stretches and says "GOOOOOD" as it prepares to set.
description.panel3: The sun dramatically says "NIIIIIIGHT" as it sets, while the moon looks on.
description.panel4: The moon, now alone in the sky, grumpily says "SHOW OFF..."

emotion: The author humorously depicts the interaction between the Sun and the Moon, expressing his dislike for those people in life who are eager to show off.

title: good morning

description.panel1: The alarm clock shows 7:00 AM and is ringing loudly.
description.panel2: A person, still in bed, groggily reaches for the alarm clock to turn it off.
description.panel3: The person accidentally falls out of bed while attempting to stop the alarm clock, hitting the table.
description.panel4: The person is shown on the floor with the alarm clock on their head, indicating a fall.

emotion: The author comically illustrates the difficulty and frustration of waking up early and dealing with an alarm clock, showcasing the clumsiness that often accompanies early morning routines.

title: Towerful.

description.panel1: Two characters are playing a game of Jenga with an unstable tower.
description.panel2: One character looks perplexed as the tower remains standing despite its instability.
description.panel3: The same character exasperatedly questions why the tower hasn't collapsed.
description.panel4: The other character, visibly stressed, compares his feelings to the unstable Jenga tower.

emotion: The author humorously uses the Jenga tower as a metaphor to convey feelings of stress and instability, illustrating how people can sometimes feel like they are on the verge of collapsing but somehow continue to stand.

title: Ancient Aliens

description.panel1: Two ancient Egyptian figures stand before a pyramid, with one figure says "You have outdone yourself this time, Babuthep!".
description.panel2: The praised figure is told they will be remembered throughout history.
description.panel3: The praised figure expresses pride, claiming the pyramid is a testament to human ingenuity.
description.panel4: A modern-day speaker at a podium tells an audience that the pyramid was built by aliens.

emotion: The author satirizes the behavior of people attributing the achievements of ancient humans to alien creatures and underestimating their intelligence.

title: Tree Class

description.panel1: A bird welcomes others to "Tree Class" and asks who is ready for fun tree facts, while another bird yawns.
description.panel2: The bird teaching the class asks if anyone can name the tree they are perched in, and one bird refers to it as the "old ugly tree."
description.panel3: The teacher bird praises a bird named Lucas for his guess but corrects him, stating that the tree is a beech. Lucas exclaims in shock about the bird swearing.
description.panel4: The teacher bird, frustrated, tells himself to stay strong and do it for the trees.

emotion: The author humorously describes the challenges and frustrations that teachers encounter in the teaching process, especially when faced with uninterested or overly rigid students, expressing a kind of praise for teachers.

title: Squelch

description.panel1: A bird excitedly tells another bird that it is finally its favorite time of the year.
description.panel2: The bird steps into a muddy puddle, causing a squelching sound.
description.panel3: The bird encourages its friend to join in the fun and squelch with it.
description.panel4: The second bird, now muddy, expresses that they do not enjoy the activity as much.

emotion: The author humorously satirizes the behavior of those who impose their own preferences on people who don't like them.

title: Find Your Niche

description.panel1: A purple character asks a blue character, "How do I get popular?"
description.panel2: The blue character advises the purple character to find their niche, explaining that they can't appeal to everyone.
description.panel3: The purple character points to a small figure named Cody and claims that their niche is "one guy named Cody."
description.panel4: The small figure named Cody enthusiastically responds, "I love you!" and the purple character replies, "Love you too, Cody!"

emotion: The author humorously describes that in the process of interpersonal communication, one should not and does not need to satisfy everyone, and the most important thing is to find people who can accept oneself.

title: Self-Care

description.panel1: A yellow character tells a blue character that it's important to practice self-care.
description.panel2: The blue character enthusiastically agrees, acknowledging the yellow character is right.
description.panel3: The blue character becomes frustrated, wondering how they missed something so obvious.
description.panel4: The blue character angrily calls themselves a stupid moron while the yellow character sweats in concern.

emotion: The author highlights the difficulty of practicing self-care, illustrating that even when people recognize its importance, they can still struggle with self-compassion and become self-critical.

title: Comeback.

description.panel1: A pink character is sleeping in bed while a blue character peeks through the window.
description.panel2: The blue character knocks on the window, causing the pink character to wake up in a startled manner.
description.panel3: The blue character tells the pink character that he have thought of a comeback to a sick burn from earlier.
description.panel4: The blue character clears his throat to deliver the comeback, while the pink character looks annoyed.

emotion: The comic humorously expresses the idea that comebacks often come too late to be effective, using the scenario of waking someone up just to share one as a way to highlight the absurdity of obsessing over past interactions.

title: Always Puppy

description.panel1: A child is lying on a bed with her puppy, while two adults look on and comment that her little puppy is growing up.
description.panel2: One of the adults is seen digging a hole in the backyard at night.
description.panel3: The adult is shown entering a store labeled "24-Hour Pets."
description.panel4: The child is happy with a new puppy, thanking her parents for her "Always Puppy."

emotion: The author expresses his desire for a happy family by describing the efforts of the parents in the cartoon to maintain the happiness of their children.

title: Crushing It

description.panel1: The squirrel asks the koala how things are going.
description.panel2: The koala responds by saying, "I'm crushing it."
description.panel3: The squirrel enthusiastically responds with "Awesome! Way to go!"
description.panel4: The koala clarifies that by "it," they mean his hopes and dreams, making the statement ironic and sad.

emotion: The author uses irony to highlight a feeling of disillusionment or failure, contrasting optimistic language with the actual negative reality of the koala's situation.

title: Chilled Out

description.panel1: Two characters express excitement upon seeing a character referred to as "Chill Phil."
description.panel2: The two characters admire Chill Phil, commenting on how chill he always seems to be.
description.panel3: Chill Phil is shown confidently smiling and maintaining his chill demeanor.
description.panel4: Chill Phil is revealed to be sweating and looking very stressed when alone.

emotion: The author highlights how appearances can be deceiving, showing that people who seem composed and unbothered on the surface might internally experience stress and anxiety.

title: adventure

description.panel1: Three characters excitedly decide to go on an adventure.
description.panel2: The three characters are joyfully running, shouting "yay" and "adventure".
description.panel3: The characters encounter a small puddle in their path and stop.
description.panel4: They turn back and walk away from the puddle, ending their adventure.

emotion: The author humorously satirizes how people often lose enthusiasm and give up when faced with small challenges.

title: sad friends

description.panel1: A person hugs a sad friend in person.
description.panel2: The same person comforts a sad friend online through a video call.
description.panel3: The person wistfully admires someone from afar who may not know they exist, feeling sad about it.
description.panel4: The person draws a comic to cheer up others who are sad.

emotion: The author expresses the varied ways one might respond to the sadness of friends and admired individuals, showing a dedication to providing comfort, even through creative means like drawing comics.

title: high-five

description.panel1: Two characters attempt a high-five, with one character saying "YEAH!"
description.panel2: The high-five attempt fails as they miss.
description.panel3: The character labeled "PAL" suggests trying the high-five again.
description.panel4: The second high-five attempt is too forceful, resulting in a "SMACK."

emotion: The author describes humorous moments of compensation between friends after failed high-fives, highlighting awkward yet endearing social interactions.

title: Forever

description.panel1: A robot asks a professor how long it will live.
description.panel2: The professor examines some documents.
description.panel3: The professor informs the robot that it looks like it is going to live forever.
description.panel4: The robot looks shocked and concerned, with the word "FOREVER?" above its head.

emotion: The author expresses his thoughts on the concept of immortality, suggesting that it may not be as desirable or simple as it seems, which may lead to feelings of worry or fear.

title: nice day

description.panel1: A person enjoys the warm, sunny weather and comments on how nice the day is.
description.panel2: The person's foot steps on a patch of ice.
description.panel3: The person slips on the ice.
description.panel4: The person falls, while the text "it hurt." appears.

emotion: The author humorously highlights how unexpected obstacles can disrupt an otherwise perfect moment, combining the themes of surprise and irony.

title: Wood You Mind.

description.panel1: A person sits on a bench holding a phone and wonders why he is mindlessly playing on it.
description.panel2: The person decides he should enjoy nature's beauty instead.
description.panel3: A tree appears to angrily speak, saying, "THE FUCK YOU LOOKIN' AT?".
description.panel4: The person looks shocked and continues to look at his phone nervously.

emotion: By describing the interaction between people and trees, the author humorously expresses that people and nature should coexist in harmony.

title: Making Musical Instruments

description.panel1: A man yawns and greets his roommate, who is busy with something out of view.
description.panel2: The roommate, along with a child, cheerfully says they are making musical instruments.
description.panel3: The yawning man looks at a stack of dirty dishes and comments on them.
description.panel4: The roommate and child happily make music with a homemade instrument while the yawning man looks on.

emotion: The author expresses how engaging in creative and fun activities can take precedence over less enjoyable tasks, showcasing the joy of making music with friends.

title: Tough Interview.

description.panel1: The interviewer asks the interviewee about his weaknesses, and the interviewee responds with "None."
description.panel2: The interviewer challenges the interviewee's response while holding a gun, and the interviewee reaffirms saying "Yep."
description.panel3: The interviewer shoots at the interviewee, but the bullet bounces off.
description.panel4: The interviewee, unaffected, suggests discussing his salary, while the interviewer looks surprised.

emotion: The author humorously and exaggeratedly illustrates the job applicant's interview scenario, where the applicant's abilities are so great that they scare the interviewer, expressing a sense of humor.

title: The Seedless Watermelon

description.panel1: Introduction of the seedless watermelon.
description.panel2: The seedless watermelon says, "If I cannot reproduce..."
description.panel3: The seedless watermelon flies towards a person, hitting them in the reproductive organs, causing pain.
description.panel4: The final frame shows the person on the ground in pain and the caption, "Another triumph for the seedless watermelon."

emotion: The cartoon satirizes people who are jealous of others because they have something that they don't.

title: Face Mask

description.panel1: A cashier announces that register 3 is open.
description.panel2: A customer approaches with their own bag, and the cashier responds approvingly.
description.panel3: The cashier thanks the customer for wearing a face mask.
description.panel4: The customer, now revealed as a robber wearing a mask, points a gun to the cashier and says "We are all in this together.", the cashier hurriedly put the money into the robber's bag.

emotion: The author uses cartoons to illustrate the many inconveniences caused by wearing masks.

title: week after easter

description.panel1: A person points out to another that Easter candy is on sale.
description.panel2: The person suggests they should get some candy.
description.panel3: The other person, now identified as Jenna, starts stacking a large amount of Easter candy in their arms.
description.panel4: Jenna has a massive stack of candy, and the other person looks concerned.

emotion: The author humorously satirizes the tendency for people to overindulge or overreact when presented with sales or discounts.

title: True Poet

description.panel1: A man enjoys the feeling of the wind running through his hair.
description.panel2: The man is described as a true poet, with a content expression on his face.
description.panel3: The man, now shown attacking another person, is revealed to also be a bully.
description.panel4: Despite bullying another person, he is reiterated to be a true poet.

emotion: The author explores the contrast and potential hypocrisy in human behavior, illustrating that a person can possess both admirable and reprehensible traits simultaneously.

title: Can You Say What I Want?

description.panel1: An adult bird asks a baby bird "Can you say "Mama"?".
description.panel2: The baby bird sits in a nest, looking up.
description.panel3: The adult bird asks the baby bird if it can say "Owl," then starts to say "maybe that's too difficu--."
description.panel4: The baby bird immediately says "Shit."

emotion: The author expresses his love for the daily life of parents and children by describing the scene of a mother bird teaching her bird children to speak.

title: Treeson

description.panel1: A tree dropped a leaf and attracted someone's attention.
description.panel2: A policeman put a "Do Not Littering" sign next to the tree and pointed at the fallen leaf.
description.panel3: The policeman found more leaves falling from the tree.
description.panel4: The policeman called for help and felt overwhelmed by the tree dropping more leaves.

emotion: The author humorously described the policeman's attitude towards the fallen leaves, hoping to trigger readers' thinking about nature and the environment.

title: Burn This City Down

description.panel1: A crow walks calmly on a sidewalk.
description.panel2: The crow suddenly tripped over a gap in the floorboards.
description.panel3: The crow's face is shown in a close-up, looking serious and determined.
description.panel4: The crow stands with a firm expression and says, "I'll burn this city down."

emotion: This cartoon humorously satirizes people who want others to share their pain.

title: Near Death Experience

description.panel1: A spirit floating away from a body informs a cloaked figure that a mistake has been made.
description.panel2: The spirit identifies itself as Tim C. Riley, not Tom C. Bailey as the list held by the cloaked figure indicates.
description.panel3: The cloaked figure, realizing an error due to forgotten glasses, prepares to correct the mistake.
description.panel4: The spirit requests to haunt their ex for fifteen minutes before being sent back.

emotion: The author mixes the serious theme of death with human desires, expressing a sense of humor that makes readers smile.

title: dressing cute

description.panel1: A character enthusiastically declares her intention to dress cutely today.
description.panel2: The character is seen happily posing in a cute outfit.
description.panel3: The character appears less confident about her cute outfit.
description.panel4: The character opts for a more practical and less cute outfit instead.

emotion: The author reflects on the experience of initially wanting to dress fashionably but ultimately choosing practicality and comfort, expressing the author's attitude of pursuing a comfortable life without caring too much about what others think.

title: Sexting

description.panel1: A person is sitting by a campfire, and smoke is forming numbers in the air.
description.panel2: The person looks up at the smoke and says, "Someone's texting me."
description.panel3: The smoke starts to form the word "yes."
description.panel4: The smoke completes the message "send nudes," and the person looks at the smoke.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the concept of sexting using an anachronistic method of communication, merging modern and traditional elements to evoke comedic effect.

title: Another Man's Treasure.

description.panel1: A person sees a raccoon eating trash and comments in surprise, to which the raccoon responds, "I am what I eat!"
description.panel2: The person, looking contemplative, says, "Damn dude..." while still observing the raccoon eating trash.
description.panel3: The person appears to have an epiphany, saying, "Raccoon... if you're trash..."
description.panel4: The person concludes, "...then I'm an ass," and the raccoon responds with "Nice," showing agreement or approval.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the idea that judging others harshly or unfairly can often reflect more poorly on the judge than on the one being judged.

title: the Great One

description.panel1: A banana exclaims that the dreaded day of judgment has arrived, addressing an apple and an orange.
description.panel2: The banana states that a sacrifice must be selected.
description.panel3: The banana raises both of its arms and says that the sacrifice is to appease "the Great One."
description.panel4: The comic reveals "the Great One" to be a blender.

emotion: The author uses humor to present the blender, essentially a common kitchen appliance, as an ominous deity for sacrificial fruits, highlighting the dramatic perspective of fruits facing blending.

title: Couldn't Hurt

description.panel1: A person holding a yellow watering can sings while approaching a potted plant.
description.panel2: The person continues to sing while pouring water from the watering can into the plant.
description.panel3: The person talks to an aloe vera plant, pondering whether it has been watered recently; the plant says "yes" and "please don't".
description.panel4: The person decides to water the aloe vera plant anyway and is berated by the plant for doing so.

emotion: Through this cartoon, the author satirizes the behavior of those who impose things they think are good on people who don't need them.

title: The Right Stuff (Remix)

description.panel1: The god says "Thinking about making bad stuff.".
description.panel2: A smaller figure asks "What is bad stuff?".
description.panel3: The smaller figure imagines and shows flying insects buzzing around.
description.panel4: The god replies "I call them wasps", to which the smaller figure reacts in horror asking "Oh god why?!"

emotion: The author humorously highlights the generally negative perception people have of wasps.

title: Weakened Warrior

description.panel1: A frog and a turtle consider using the weekend to catch up on work.
description.panel2: The frog and the turtle also consider using the weekend to recuperate.
description.panel3: On the following Monday, the frog asks the turtle what it ended up doing.
description.panel4: The turtle responds that it ended up doing neither.

emotion: The author expresses a common dilemma of balancing work and rest and how indecision or procrastination can lead to accomplishing neither goal.

title: Praise Be

description.panel1: A divine figure closes a door while standing next to a confused person.
description.panel2: The divine figure points towards an open window showing an alternative.
description.panel3: The person is shocked to discover a swarm of bees coming through the window.
description.panel4: The divine figure calmly acknowledges the bees.

emotion: The author humorously illustrates that when one opportunity closes and another one opens, the new opportunity may come with unexpected or problematic challenges.

title: New Year's Resolutions

description.panel1: The character excitedly looks at their New Year's resolutions to check off items they've accomplished.
description.panel2: The character reads through their list of resolutions for 2015 with a puzzled expression.
description.panel3: The character shrugs, believing they might have forgotten to include something.
description.panel4: The list reveals the character has only accomplished "EAT A LOT OF CHEESE," which is confidently checked off.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the gap between ideals and reality by describing the reality of making ambitious New Year's resolutions but only achieving the simplest goals.

title: wigged out

description.panel1: A character places a wig to dry on a surface and says "I will just leave this wig here to dry".
description.panel2: The character reassures herself that she won't forget the wig while she get distracted by looking at something on the computer.
description.panel3: Later, the character is carrying an item and feels like she is forgetting something.
description.panel4: The character realizes she have forgotten the wig, which remains on the surface as they exclaim "WHYS AHEAD".

emotion: The author expresses a kind reminder to readers by humorously describing the common human experience of distraction and forgetfulness.

title: Snowflake

description.panel1: A person in an orange shirt is sitting on a bench and thinks, "They say, a home is not a place but a person."
description.panel2: The person in the orange shirt continues thinking, "If that's true..."
description.panel3: The person in the orange shirt sees another person who appears to be homeless and concludes, "I'm pretty much homeless."
description.panel4: The homeless person is seen drinking, while another person walks by and comments, "Great story, snowflake."

emotion: The author expresses the supporting role of emotions in people by describing the characters in the comics' thoughts on family.

title: Bedmate

description.panel1: A character is sitting at a desk with a computer, thinking, "They say absence makes the heart grow fonder."
description.panel2: The character appears more distraught and thinks, "But I can't stand being away from you..."
description.panel3: The character holds a photo frame and says, "I'll see you soon, baby."
description.panel4: The photo frame reveals a picture of a bed.

emotion: The author humorously conveys the idea that sometimes, the comfort and familiarity of one's bed can be deeply missed, playfully representing it with exaggerated affection.

title: Having the Gull

description.panel1: The green character holding the falcon boasts that his falcon hunts game for he.
description.panel2: The pink character states that "My seagull swiped a half-eaten bag of chips for me.".
description.panel3: The seagull angrily shouts at the pink character, telling "These chips are mine.".
description.panel4: The green character watched speechlessly as the pink character argued with the seagull.

emotion: The author uses the contrast between these two characters to cleverly satirize those who like to make meaningless comparisons.

title: How It's Going.

description.panel1: A blue character asks a pink character how things are going, and the pink character responds with "GREAT!"
description.panel2: The blue character asks for clarification about what the pink character meant by "it," and the pink character specifies "your life."
description.panel3: The pink character, appearing thoughtful, says that his life is going horribly.
description.panel4: The pink character then elaborates that his life is an utterly horrendous situation, likened to a "dog shit dumpster fire."

emotion: The author illustrates the contrast between a superficial positive response and the stark reality of someone's difficult circumstances, highlighting the tendency for people to mask their true feelings behind socially acceptable answers.

title: Good or Bad.

description.panel1: A person is lying on a couch while an angel and a devil are suggesting that they do something good or something bad, respectively.
description.panel2: The person contemplates another option and suggests doing absolutely nothing instead.
description.panel3: The person continues lying on the couch while the angel and devil hover nearby.
description.panel4: The devil expresses frustration using profanity, to which the angel reacts by scolding for the language.

emotion: Through the choices made by the characters in the comic, the author shows that he only wants to live a leisurely lifestyle.

title: Take Me Again

description.panel1: A person eagerly raises their arms towards a hovering UFO, saying "I knew you'd come to take me."
description.panel2: The person is now on their knees and says, "Come on, take me already," as the UFO continues to hover.
description.panel3: Inside the UFO, two aliens look in the person's direction through a window.
description.panel4: The UFO leaves, and the person remains on their knees, seemingly disappointed.

emotion: The author expresses a feeling of unmet expectations gradually turning into disappointment, and emphasizes that people should stick to their own hearts.

title: Masterpiece.

description.panel1: Two pumpkins are on the ground, and one of them is hoping to be picked so it can become a cool-looking jack-o'-lantern.
description.panel2: A hand reaches out and picks up the hopeful pumpkin, which is thrilled at the prospect of becoming a masterpiece.
description.panel3: The pumpkin is now in the hands of a person who looks nervous and is about to carve it with a knife.
description.panel4: The carved pumpkin sits on the steps, looking disappointed with its appearance.

emotion: The author expresses the theme of unmet expectations and the gap between anticipation and reality, highlighting how outcomes can sometimes fall short of our hopes.

title: Spider

description.panel1: A fork and a tiny spider are shown, with lyrics "The itsy-bitsy spider climbed up the water spout” indicating the beginning of a nursery rhyme.
description.panel2: The spider is near the fork, and the lyrics "Down came the rain and washed the spider out" continue the nursery rhyme.
description.panel3: A close-up of a larger spider appears, laughing and saying "HAHA!".
description.panel4: The larger spider continues, saying "Nah… When it rains we just come into your houses".

emotion: Through the spider's reaction, the author emphasizes that people should learn to be flexible in their daily lives.

title: Thoughtful

description.panel1: Two women greet each other in the morning.
description.panel2: One of the women accuses the other of not flushing the toilet after using it.
description.panel3: The accused woman explains that she didn't flush because she didn't want to wake the other woman, who was sleeping.
description.panel4: The accused woman, with tears in her eyes, says she was just being thoughtful.

emotion: By describing the conflict between the two ladies, the author expresses the helpless feeling that in daily life, people always care about others in their own way, but sometimes this may cause misunderstandings from others.

title: Charging

description.panel1: The robot is joyfully frolicking in a field, saying "Weeeee! I love frolicking."
description.panel2: The robot begins to slump as it says “Weeee... Battery low.”
description.panel3: The robot lies face down on the ground, having run out of battery.
description.panel4: The robot lifts its solar panel towards the sun, recharging itself while saying "Charging... Charging..."

emotion: The author expresses the importance of energy by describing the robot's behavior.

title: Life is Disappointing

description.panel1: A bird perched on a branch of a flowering tree says, "Life is like a flower; beautiful but fragile."
description.panel2: The bird tugs on one of the flowers with its beak.
description.panel3: The bird continues to tug on the flower, becoming more forceful.
description.panel4: Close-up of the bird's angry face.

emotion: The author likely aims to juxtapose the bird's initial philosophical musing about life's fragility with its eventual frustration, suggesting a humorous contrast between idealistic thoughts and challenging, frustrating experiences in reality.

title: Fun House

description.panel1: A clown welcomes a person to a building labeled "Fun House."
description.panel2: A close-up of the sign on the building shows the word "Murder" crossed out and replaced with "Fun."
description.panel3: The person asks the clown why the word "Murder" is crossed out.
description.panel4: The clown explains that his marketing manager believes "fun" works better with his key demographic.

emotion: The author highlights how marketing strategies are often superficial and sometimes misleading, satirizing how they prioritize appealing to a target demographic over honesty and transparency.

title: Howl

description.panel1: The moon appears to be howling with a speech bubble saying "AWOOOOOO".
description.panel2: The moon is shown silent and contemplative.
description.panel3: The moon repeats its howl, "AWOOOOOO", which echoes across the landscape.
description.panel4: A dog is seen howling back in response to the moon.

emotion: By exchanging the roles between the moon and the dog, the author expresses a sense of humor and makes the readers laugh.

title: Make Nice

description.panel1: A pink character wishes a yellow character a nice day, and the yellow character responds that they will try.
description.panel2: Later at night, the pink character wakes up the yellow character by whispering "pssst" at the window.
description.panel3: The pink character says they are just checking to see if the yellow character had a nice day, while the yellow character looks surprised and tired.
description.panel4: The yellow character confirms they did have a nice day, and the pink character happily responds, "Oh, marvelous."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the extreme lengths one might go to in order to ensure someone else is having a good day, emphasizing a comical twist on being overly caring or concerned.

title: Nobody ever cares

description.panel1: A bird is sniffing the air while standing on a branch.
description.panel2: The bird steps on a thorn and says "OW".
description.panel3: The bird looks away, continuing to deal with the pain.
description.panel4: Another bird on the branch tells the first bird, "Nobody cares, Sam".

emotion: Through the interaction between the two birds, the author describes the phenomenon that people lack sympathy or care in social interactions, and hopes that people can care more about each other.

title: hop hop hop hop

description.panel1: A bird is seen hopping along the train tracks.
description.panel2: The bird looks surprised as a loud "HONK" sound is depicted.
description.panel3: A train is approaching, making loud noises as it moves down the tracks.
description.panel4: The bird continues hopping away from the train, seemingly unbothered by the noise.

emotion: The author expresses the idea of resilience or indifference in the face of potentially dangerous or disruptive situations, as the bird continues its behavior despite the approaching train.

title: So It Goes

description.panel1: A squirrel asks a koala how he has been.
description.panel2: The koala replies that he has been "eh, so so.”
description.panel3: The squirrel expresses sympathy, saying, "Sorry to hear that."
description.panel4: The koala explains that "so so" is actually the best he has felt in months.

emotion: The author addresses people's concern about how to improve their well-being by describing the best feeling the koalas have felt in months as "so-so".

title: waiting

description.panel1: A person sitting on a couch says, "They should be here anytime now."
description.panel2: The same person, now lying down and looking at their phone, repeats, "Yep, anytime... yep."
description.panel3: The person, now sitting upright, looks at the phone and says "anytime..." with an orange sky outside.
description.panel4: The person stares out the window at night, questioning, "...right?"

emotion: The author expresses the anxiety and uncertainty that comes with waiting for someone who does not arrive on time, highlighting the passage of time and the growing doubt that accompanies prolonged waiting.

title: Face to Face

description.panel1: A person standing with a hand on their hip says, "Alright... WHO POOPED ON THE FLOOR?" while another person with a cat on their lap looks on.
description.panel2: The person's face shows an uneasy expression.
description.panel3: The same person's face is shown again in close-up, looking increasingly uncomfortable and guilty.
description.panel4: A close-up of the cat's face, looking indifferent and unconcerned.

emotion: This cartoon describes a common thing in the process of raising a pet cat and expresses the author's complex emotions towards pet cats.

title: Scary Costume

description.panel1: A child sitting at a table says, "This year for Halloween...".
description.panel2: The child continues, "...I wanna go as something really scary!" while the ghost listens.
description.panel3: The ghost then says, "I have an idea."
description.panel4: The child is launched through the air wearing a gory, disheveled outfit that frightens nearby adults.

emotion: The author humorously and exaggeratedly describes how the scary appearance of children on Halloween has an extremely funny effect on adults, expressing people's love for Halloween.

title: Doctor Dog

description.panel1: A doctor is in the operating room, and a doctor calls out urgently: "Doctor Dog! We have to operate!"
description.panel2: A nurse says: "Hurry up, Doctor Dog! We don't have much time!"
description.panel3: Doctor Dog is tangled up in his tail and says: "Wait a minute, this guy has been following me all day!"
description.panel4: Doctor Dog stands there, staring out the window with a distant look, and the sign on the floor reads "Medical Malpractice Lawsuit".

emotion: The author humorously describes how one's own habits or behaviors can lead to the failure of what is being done, and warns people to overcome certain behaviors when doing important things.

title: No Kidding

description.panel1: A character labeled "GOV" angrily asks another character "Why won't you have kids".
description.panel2: The second character points at a thermometer indicating extremely high temperatures.
description.panel3: The second character points at an air quality chart, showing the quality as "UNSAFE."
description.panel4: The second character stands in a hellish environment with flames, further indicating harsh living conditions.

emotion: This cartoon satirizes the fact that the government only cares about the birth rate but does not create a good living environment for the people.

title: Berries

description.panel1: A bird is perched on a tree branch.
description.panel2: The bird looks around and says, "huh."
description.panel3: The bird is hanging upside down on this tree.
description.panel4: The bird has a woozy expression and says, "I think those berries were fermented."

emotion: The author humorously describes the bird becoming drunk and disoriented after eating fermented berries, expressing a humorous emotion.

title: An Asshole

description.panel1: A bird initiates a pecking action towards another bird.
description.panel2: The second bird reacts in alarm and asks the first bird to stop.
description.panel3: The second bird contemplates its mortality and the legacy it will leave behind.
description.panel4: The second bird resolves to improve its behavior by stopping being rude.

emotion: The author conveys a message about self-reflection and the decision to improve one's behavior to create a more positive impact on others.

title: Days are Numbered.

description.panel1: A student frustrated with math is told by another character, "Who cares if I suck at math?" with the response, "You'll see."
description.panel2: A sign reads "Years Later..." with the same character older, playing video games.
description.panel3: An IRS agent bursts into the room and says, "IRS! Your math sucked!"
description.panel4: The character is shown behind bars, looking regretful and sad.

emotion: The author highlights the importance of education and mastery of basic subjects by describing the serious consequences of neglecting important skills such as mathematics.

title: The Lord's Plan

description.panel1: A man with a cross necklace tells Andy that the Lord has a plan for everyone.
description.panel2: The man explains that the Lord's plan for Paul is to spread the gospel, and Andy nods in agreement.
description.panel3: The man points towards Andy, saying that Andy's plan is to say the punchline for the comic, which causes Andy to look taken aback.
description.panel4: Andy stands nervously under a spotlight, looking uncertain.

emotion: The author humorously ridiculed himself for the fact that his comics were not funny at all, demonstrating his hard work and ambition.

title: strong vision

description.panel1: A person asks their friend if they can try on their glasses, despite the friend warning about their bad eyesight.
description.panel2: The person eagerly puts on the glasses while the friend protests.
description.panel3: The person pauses, appearing to experience discomfort from wearing the glasses.
description.panel4: The person exclaims in pain, returning the glasses and questioning "how's your vision so bad?"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the drastic difference in vision quality between individuals and the discomfort experienced when someone with normal vision tries on someone else's strong prescription glasses.

title: Shut up

description.panel1: A dog asks another dog if he has ever considered that smelling butts might be wrong.
description.panel2: The dog behind him tells the questioning dog to shut up.
description.panel3: The dog reiterates to the questioning dog to keep quiet.
description.panel4: Most of the dogs start sniffing the other dogs’ butts.

emotion: The author humorously highlights how social norms or behaviors may be questioned by some, but are often firmly defended and followed by the majority.

title: Flippant

description.panel1: A pink character observes a green object (a turtle) on the ground.
description.panel2: The pink character, with a confident expression, says to the turtle, "you look like you could use some help, little guy".
description.panel3: The pink character walks away, leaving the turtle behind.
description.panel4: The turtle remains lying on the ground alone.

emotion: The author satires the superficial and insincere nature of some offers of help, where the helper has no genuine intention of actually assisting.

title: Draw A Deer

description.panel1: The panel shows the title "How to Draw A Deer."
description.panel2: A simple outline of a deer with basic shapes is depicted.
description.panel3: The image of the deer is further detailed with texture and form.
description.panel4: The completed detailed deer is shown saying, "Free will is an illusion."

emotion: The author uses the drawing process to humorously convey a philosophical message about determinism, suggesting that what appears to be a creative or free expression is actually pre-determined.

title: Cheat Sheet

description.panel1: A green character bursts into a room, saying they forgot something, while a person on the bed looks startled.
description.panel2: The green character reaches inside the room while saying they forgot their wallet, and the person in the bed watches nervously.
description.panel3: The green character retrieves the wallet and wishes the person on the bed a nice day, to which the person responds nervously.
description.panel4: The person on the bed, now revealed to be a yellow character disguised under a wig and blanket, comments that it was a close call.

emotion: The author describes the scene of escaping death and the tension of the pretender who was almost caught in a humorous way, expressing a sense of humor.

title: hello ...

description.panel1: A person is walking, and another figure appears in front of him, waving at him.
description.panel2: The first person also waves at him, but the second person has already walked past the first person.
description.panel3: The first person faces backward, with a slightly awkward expression.
description.panel4: The first person continues to face backward, looking sad and lonely.

emotion: The author shows a sense of humor by describing the illusion of thinking that others are greeting him in daily life, which makes readers who have experienced the same experience smile.

title: It Is Good To Be Alive

description.panel1: A rabbit expresses delight by saying "What a beautiful fucking day".
description.panel2: The rabbit continues its appreciation by stating "Nothing beats this shit".
description.panel3: Observing a flower, the rabbit comments "Look at that fucking flower".
description.panel4: The rabbit sums up the feelings by saying "Shit, it's good to be alive".

emotion: The author uses humor to express his love for life.

title: Narcissism

description.panel1: An eagle flies in the sky with a speech bubble saying "HOW."
description.panel2: The eagle descends towards a branch, saying "AM."
description.panel3: The eagle lands on the branch, holding its wings open with  a speech bubble saying "I."
description.panel4: A close-up of the eagle's face shows it with a confident expression, and its speech bubble says, "SO GREAT."

emotion: The author uses cartoons to humorously explain the concept of narcissism and satirizes people in life who are very vain and often brag about themselves.

title: Insignific-ant

description.panel1: A blue figure looks down sadly and says, "I'm as insignificant as an ant," while looking at an ant and a leaf on the ground.
description.panel2: The ant, holding the leaf, responds with, "Speak for yourself!"
description.panel3: The ant lifts the leaf above its head, exclaiming, "HIYA!"
description.panel4: The blue figure and two other characters applaud the ant’s strength and effort.

emotion: The author presents the idea that even those perceived as small or insignificant can possess remarkable strength and capabilities.

title: Boobs

description.panel1: A person in bed hears a distant "Booo!" sound.
description.panel2: The person in bed hears a louder "Booooooo!" sound.
description.panel3: The person in bed hears an even louder "Boooooobs!" sound.
description.panel4: The person, now out of bed, finds another character at the door who innocently asks, "Boobs?"

emotion: The author humorously tells how a terrible or shocking scene suddenly turns into a ridiculous and lighthearted misunderstanding, which makes people smile.

description.panel1: A rocket is launched into the sky, wishing everyone a happy new year.
description.panel2: The rocket ascends higher into the sky, expressing discomfort about the height.
description.panel3: The rocket feels queasy and says it might throw up.
description.panel4: The rocket explodes into fireworks, illuminating the sky.

emotion: The author humorously personifies the rocket to depict a typical New Year's firework, showcasing its journey and ultimate explosion in a lighthearted and relatable manner.Provides a sense of humor to readers.

title: Giant Cock

description.panel1: A girl sends a text message to her mother saying she is having fun riding her boyfriend's giant cock.
description.panel2: The image shows the girl riding a giant rooster (cock).
description.panel3: The mother reads the message and replies, asking the girl to say hi to Bob from her.
description.panel4: The final panel shows a ranch with a sign saying "Bob's Giant Cock Ranch" and a man standing in front of it.

emotion: The author uses a play on words to create a humorous misunderstanding that plays on the dual meanings of the word "cock," showcasing how context can drastically change the interpretation of a message.

title: Spring Chicken

description.panel1: A character shouts with joy that they can finally enjoy the outdoors again.
description.panel2: A bee responds, "Me too."
description.panel3: The character immediately returns to his house.
description.panel4: The house is boarded up, and the character peeks out through the boards.

emotion: The author humorously depicts the extreme fear or discomfort some people may have with bees, which may lead them to take extreme measures to avoid encountering bees outside, and the author expresses sympathy and concern for such people.

title: sports

description.panel1: A person is standing among a crowd, thinking to themselves that they are going to be chosen last.
description.panel2: The person looks concerned as the crowd diminishes, questioning why this is happening.
description.panel3: The person realizes there are only two people left, and they hope they might not be chosen last.
description.panel4: The person is disappointed as one of the choosers suggests taking both remaining individuals, resulting in the person not even being chosen last.

emotion: The author describes the insecurity, anxiety, and disappointment that comes from the fear of being chosen, and how these feelings are exacerbated by being chosen last.

title: Way to use your head!

description.panel1: A person in a soccer uniform is jogging and thinking, "Soccer isn't nearly as scary as I remember it from my childhood!"
description.panel2: A close-up of the face of this person.
description.panel3: The person is hit in the face with a soccer ball.
description.panel4: The person's facial structure is distorted and crushed by the impact of the soccer ball.

emotion: The author satirizes those who have a lucky mentality by having the people in the cartoon being attacked by a football.

title: Giant Leap

description.panel1: A person at a podium says, "One small step for man, one giant leap for poledancing," next to a "Moon Land" poster.
description.panel2: An audience member raises a hand and asks, "Don't you mean for mankind?"
description.panel3: The person at the podium responds with, "No."
description.panel4: An astronaut plants a pole with an American flag on the moon, preparing to dance on it.

emotion: The author presents the famous moon landing quote with humor and unexpected twists that will make you smile.

title: Cat School

description.panel1: A cat instructs a class of cats "To maximize cuteness, we roll on our back and present the fluffy belly".
description.panel2: One of the cats in the class raises a paw and asks "When they pet us, what do we do ? ".
