description.panel3: The instructor cat responds, "Excellent question."
description.panel4: The instructor cat explains that "As soon as the human takes the bait, we should claw them".

emotion: The author humorously highlights the cat's playful and naughty behavior, expressing a kind of helplessness and love for cats.

title: Home is Where the Heart is

description.panel1: A frog tells a turtle that he might be homeless soon.
description.panel2: The turtle responds by saying the frog's home is right on his back.
description.panel3: The turtle sarcastically questions if his shell has amenities like running water, power, and heating.
description.panel4: The turtle adds more amenities like cooling, plumbing, internet, appliances, and furniture, to which the frog resignedly replies "Okay."

emotion: The author employs humor to show the contrast in perspectives about what constitutes a home, highlighting a more literal and simplistic view versus a more complex, modern understanding of what a home entails.

title: Not Today, Tomorrow

description.panel1: The turtle says "I have so much to do tomorrow.".
description.panel2: The frog suggests that "You could do some of it today".
description.panel3: The turtle responds with a simple "No."
description.panel4: The turtle states that "I won't let a tomorrow ruin a perfectly good today.".

emotion: The author uses the turtle's words to express a feeling of enjoying the present moment, which makes people think about the relationship between work and life.

title: Pour Heart Out

description.panel1: A yellow character asks other characters, "I am grabbing a drink. You want anything?".
description.panel2: A black menacing character requests "Fresh blood drained from a pure heart".
description.panel3: The yellow character then asks another blue character "What about you ben?".
description.panel4: The blue character responds indecisively that he want the same thing as the black character.

emotion: The author uses the contrast between the blue characters and the black characters to create a ridiculous laugh.

title: Hey Baby

description.panel1: A bee approaches a tulip and says, "Hey baby..."
description.panel2: The bee continues speaking to the tulip, asking, "Did it hurt when you fell from heaven?"
description.panel3: A butterfly emerges from the tulip, revealing that it was there instead of the bee's intended subject.
description.panel4: The bee flies away, appearing dejected.

emotion: The author humorously portrays the missteps and awkward moments that can occur in the world of flirting, suggesting that sometimes attempts at romance don't go as planned.

title: Inaction Enaction

description.panel1: A frog expresses a desire to complete a small task.
description.panel2: A turtle questions why the frog would do the task immediately.
description.panel3: The turtle suggests letting the task worry the frog and degrade their mood instead.
description.panel4: The frog responds sarcastically, saying that the turtle's suggestion sounds better.

emotion: The author is highlighting the irrationality of procrastination and how it unnecessarily adds stress and negatively impacts one's mood.

title: Beautiful Sadness

description.panel1: A person is sitting on a chair, holding a book and saying that the book makes them sad.
description.panel2: The person states that the book makes them want to cry and hug themselves.
description.panel3: The person expresses that the book gives them the feeling of eternal solitude.
description.panel4: The person concludes by saying that they love the book.

emotion: The author expresses the complex nature of emotional experiences, highlighting how individuals can find beauty and enjoyment in sadness and solitude.

title: Living Nightmare

description.panel1: A green character says "Hello and welcome to life!" to a blue character with a worried expression.
description.panel2: The green character tells the blue character, "Before you is an indefinite span of work, consumption, and social obligations!"
description.panel3: The blue character wakes up in bed, sweating and shouting "NOOO!!"
description.panel4: The blue character lies back down, relieved, and says, "Oh thank God, it was just a dream. I'm still dead."

emotion: The author humorously expresses the anxiety and burden that can come with life’s responsibilities, suggesting that for some, the idea of eternal rest might be more appealing than dealing with the pressures of daily life.

title: Hound Soothe.

description.panel1: A purple character is angrily questioning a small dog that is wiggling on the ground, asking, "WHAT ARE YOU DOING?!"
description.panel2: The dog, now lying on its back, responds introspectively with, "WHAT ARE ANY OF US DOING?"
description.panel3: The purple character appears contemplative with a blank expression.
description.panel4: Both the purple character and the dog are now wiggling together on the ground.

emotion: The author uses the before and after comparison of the purple character to show that people often think happiness is complicated, but in fact happiness is very simple.

title: Regrudge

description.panel1: A woman named Anna enthusiastically greets a man named Eli, indicating it has been a long time since they last met.
description.panel2: Anna asks Eli if he remembers a disagreement they had five years ago, to which Eli responds that he doesn’t remember.
description.panel3: Anna explains that she has thought about their disagreement every night and has come up with good counter-arguments.
description.panel4: Anna mentions that she would like to start by reading an opening statement she has prepared.

emotion: The author uses contrast to illustrate that some people may be obsessed with minor conflicts while another party may have completely forgotten about them, expressing the difference in each person’s outlook on life.

title: About Face

description.panel1: The main character sits with a smile, showing off their flawless skin while working on a laptop.
description.panel2: The main character is still sitting at their desk when their phone buzzes with a new message.
description.panel3: The main character picks up the phone and sees a message from their crush saying "come over."
description.panel4: The main character, now his face covered in acne, looks anxious as they look at their phone.

emotion: The author expresses his longing for love by describing how he can't help but feel nervous when chatting with someone he likes.

title: Eating a Whole Tree

description.panel1: One bird tells another that despite feeling small and insignificant, there is something to consider.
description.panel2: The first bird points out that each seed in the cones could grow into a massive tree.
description.panel3: The second bird expresses disappointment, stating it’s not a tree or a seed and will never be massive.
description.panel4: The first bird reveals that by eating a seed, it's like consuming a whole tree, shocking the second bird.

emotion: Through the dialogue between the two birds, the author expresses his thoughts on value and meaning, emphasizing that everyone has great potential and importance.

title: Haunting Work

description.panel1: A bird speaks, saying "Winter isn't so bad."
description.panel2: The bird continues, saying "It gives me time to take risks in my art."
description.panel3: The bird displays a picture it has drawn, which depicts a haunting and surreal bird-like figure made with different elements.
description.panel4: The bird, next to the displayed picture, comments "My most haunting work to date."

emotion: By describing the bird's "haunting work", the author expresses that even if the external environment is not good, we must maintain a positive attitude.

title: No Network

description.panel1: A person looks at a computer screen displaying "NOPE" and comments that the internet is down.
description.panel2: The person asks a cat, named Lady, what he should do now.
description.panel3: The cat, sitting by a window, has a spinning loading icon over its head.
description.panel4: The person remarks that cats don't work without the internet, addressing Josh.

emotion: The author humorously expresses how reliant people—and even pets, in a joking manner—have become on the internet for functioning and passing time.

title: A Great Mind

description.panel1: A bird is flying next to an intricate, decorative building and exclaims "Visionary!"
description.panel2: The bird is flying next to a plain, utilitarian bridge and exclaims "Functional!"
description.panel3: The bird is now next to an ancient, weathered building ruin and exclaims "Timeless!"
description.panel4: The bird is sitting in a well-crafted nest on a tree branch, saying "Only a great mind can do all three."

emotion: The author conveys that true talent is the ability to combine visionary design, functionality, and timelessness into one’s creations.

title: Tall Order

description.panel1: A server brings a very tall burger to a table with one surprised customer.
description.panel2: The customer questions how he is supposed to eat the excessively tall burger.
description.panel3: The server goes to the kitchen, reporting that the customers said the burger is too big for their mouths.
description.panel4: A chef, who appears to be a snake, asks the server if they advised the customers to unhinge their jaws, and the server confirms they did.

emotion: The author humorously satirizes those who cannot recognize the differences between themselves and others.

title: Nest Egg.

description.panel1: A character says, "I'm finally retired. Let's see what's in my nest egg," while looking at an egg in a nest.
description.panel2: The egg begins to crack open, making rustling sounds.
description.panel3: An angry-looking bird emerges from the cracked egg.
description.panel4: The bird, now fully out of the egg, says to the character, "You're fucked!", causing the character to cry.

emotion: The author expressed the uncertainty and potential disappointment of relying on a "nest egg" for financial security in retirement.

title: caring about not caring

description.panel1: A diligent student who is 15 is studying hard, thinking "GOTTA DO MY BEST!".
description.panel2: The same student who is 16 receives a paper or grade, now with a relaxed expression and thinking "B's ARE ALRIGHT."
description.panel3: The student who is 17 is now lounging on a couch playing video games, thinking "C's ARE PASSING..."
description.panel4: The student who is 18 is crying over several papers, apparently overwhelmed.

emotion: The author illustrates how academic motivation can diminish over time and how initial high aspirations can lead to disappointment when standards are gradually lowered.

title: Foreshadowing...

description.panel1: Two people are on public transportation, and the man draws attention with an enthusiastic gesture saying, "Hey hey, look!"
description.panel2: The man points to a sign that reads "Assistance" and makes a pun by saying, "Do you need help? Cuz your "ass is tense"! Haha! Get it?"
description.panel3: He laughs heartily and responds with a joyful "Hahahaa...yes!", but the woman looks unhappy.
description.panel4: The man checks off "Make the world a happier place" from a to-do list.

emotion: The author satirizes those who like to make inappropriate jokes and think they bring happiness to others.

title: Styrofoam

description.panel1: A seagull tells another seagull that "I do not understand anything, really, so I like to be skeptical about everything.".
description.panel2: The second seagull remarks that its stomach hurts after eating styrofoam.
description.panel3: The first seagull wonders if styrofoam might actually be good for it.
description.panel4: The second seagull responds that the first seagull’s skepticism sounds like ignorance, and the first seagull agrees, speculating that it might not know what healthy feels like.

emotion: The author satirizes skepticism and ignorance while also reminding everyone of the importance of protecting the environment.

title: Trash

description.panel1: A larger mountain comments that "There's trash everywhere.".
description.panel2: A smaller mountain informs the larger mountain that the "trash" is actually humans.
description.panel3: The larger mountain remains silent, processing the information.
description.panel4: The larger mountain reaffirms its previous statement implying humans are the trash.

emotion: This cartoon humorously satirizes the great pressure that humans put on the environment through a dialogue between two mountains.

title: Incessant Replay

description.panel1: The brain is reviewing a past conversation on a screen, noting the mix-up between "How's it going?" and "What's up?" as a terrible start.
description.panel2: The brain questions why the people in the conversation are yelling.
description.panel3: The person asks if they really need to scrutinize the conversation, to which the brain sarcastically responds, asking if they enjoy being socially inept.
description.panel4: The brain suggests skipping ahead to the awkward hug at the end of the conversation.

emotion: By describing the anxiety and self-criticism that often accompanies rumination on past social missteps, the author conveys the message that people should not be too hard on themselves for minor social errors.

title: Keep Your Eye on the Ball

description.panel1: A character lying in bed asks their boss over the phone if he really need to come into work today.
description.panel2: The boss responds that a giant, inter-planetary eyeball in the sky doesn't stop work.
description.panel3: The boss sternly tells the character to get in or be fired.
description.panel4: The character drives a car with the enormous eyeball visible in the sky behind him.

emotion: The author expresses his own exhaustion and sarcasm towards his boss by describing the situation where he has to work as usual even when special circumstances arise.

title: Not Like This

description.panel1: A person sitting on a toilet is startled by the sudden appearance of the Grim Reaper, exclaiming "Oh no, not like this!"
description.panel2: The Grim Reaper, in response, says "OK, I'll wait," and begins to leave the bathroom.
description.panel3: The Grim Reaper peeks back in to ask, "Number one or number two?"
description.panel4: The Grim Reaper is seen sitting and reading a magazine, waiting outside the bathroom.

emotion: The comic humorously depicts frightening encounters with the Grim Reaper in awkward situations and unexpected twists, expressing a sense of humor.

title: Changing

description.panel1: A young man with a baby face and text that says "BABYFACE SHEEP."
description.panel2: The same man, looking slightly older, touching his face with the text "TOING."
description.panel3: The man, now older, has the text "DISAPPOINTMENT."
description.panel4: The man, now aged, with grey hair and a mustache, saying "JUST GIVE IT A FEW DECADES, BOYS!"

emotion: The author humorously illustrates the common experience of underestimating the effects of onion steam when cutting onions, humorously expressing that one can only believe something if one actually experiences it.

title: I Can Do This

description.panel1: A person is telling Josh not to let bullies affect him.
description.panel2: Josh decides to reclaim his space, saying he can do it.
description.panel3: Josh encounters aggressive geese that hiss and startle him.
description.panel4: Josh faces the geese with a determined yet wary expression.

emotion: The author highlights how despite initial confidence and determination, facing daunting challenges can still be faltering.

title: The Good Stuff

description.panel1: A person in a trench coat whispers to another person, "PSST PAL, LOOKS LIKE YOU COULD USE..."
description.panel2: The same person in the trench coat spreads their arms wide and says, "A HUG."
description.panel3: The two people are seen hugging warmly.
description.panel4: While still hugging, the person in the trench coat says, "THAT'LL BE 20 BUCKS."

emotion: The author gives a humorous and unexpected interpretation to this seemingly well-intentioned gesture, satirizing the commercialization of sympathy.

title: Reunion

description.panel1: A character is in the afterlife and acknowledges it by saying, "So this is the afterlife."
description.panel2: The character sees Hopes and recognizes it, asking, "Hopes? Hopes, is that you?"
description.panel3: The character also sees Dreams and is visibly emotional, saying, "And Dreams! Oh, it's been so long."
description.panel4: Another character (presumably the character's husband) appears and warns, "Anna, it's me, your husband," while the main character reacts emotionally, shouting, "Stay away from them!"

emotion: By describing this character's attitude toward her husband, the author prompts readers to think about the relationship between women's family, dreams, and hopes.

title: Breaking

description.panel1: A character expresses feeling happy.
description.panel2: The character wonders if he might be forgetting something.
description.panel3: A "News" character bursts in and exclaims that everything is bad (using exaggerated language).
description.panel4: The original character realizes what he had forgotten and acknowledges it, feeling silly.

emotion: The author describes how a constant stream of negative news can overshadow moments of happiness, satirizing how the pervasive negativity in the media can make feelings of happiness seem foreign or inappropriate.

title: when long hair is nice

description.panel1: A person with long hair feels awkward and fidgety, using their hair as a way to cope.
description.panel2: The person feels self-conscious and hides behind their long hair.
description.panel3: The person feels like hiding from the world's cruelty and uses their hair to cover their face.
description.panel4: Another person advises the person to cut their hair, but the long-haired person looks even more threatened and hides further.

emotion: The author expresses the idea that long hair can serve as a personal shield for those who feel vulnerable, and that advising someone to change this aspect of their appearance can exacerbate their feelings of discomfort and threat.

title: Cold Weather

description.panel1: An elderly woman comments on the chilly weather while a younger person standing nearby responds that it is November.
description.panel2: The elderly woman seems irritated and says "EH?!".
description.panel3: The elderly woman repeats her comment more angrily, saying "IT SURE IS CHILLY, EH?!".
description.panel4: The younger person, looking frightened, nervously laughs and comments again on the weather.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the differences in concepts between people of different ages, reminding us that we should pay attention to this in life.

title: alarming

description.panel1: The character examines a "sound therapy alarm clock" and notes its various settings.
description.panel2: The character decides to set the alarm clock to the "Tropical" sound setting.
description.panel3: The character hopes that the alarm will actually wake them up and goes to sleep.
description.panel4: The character is abruptly woken up by a loud "SCREEECH" sound from the alarm.

emotion: The author humorously expresses the idea that sometimes alternative solutions to problems, such as using a sound therapy alarm clock, can lead to unexpected and unpleasant results.

title: Lucky Chickens

description.panel1: A chicken is laughing and saying "HA HA."
description.panel2: The chicken is excitedly running and shouting "WHEEEEEE!"
description.panel3: The chicken, standing still, remarks, "BOY! IN THIS KOOKY WORLD OF OURS..."
description.panel4: A close-up of the chicken's face as it says, "I SURE AM LUCKY TO BE A CHICKEN!"

emotion: The author conveys a light-hearted and humorous perspective on life through the chicken's cheerful and positive attitude, emphasizing gratitude for one's current existence.

title: Say Your Prayers.

description.panel1: A blue character points a gun at a pink character and instructs them to pray.
description.panel2: The pink character clasps their hands together in prayer, while the blue character keeps the gun pointed at them.
description.panel3: The blue character approves of the pink character's prayer and tells them they will see them next Sunday.
description.panel4: An advertisement for "GUN PRIEST" is shown, indicating there will be more of this series next week.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to juxtapose the sacred act of prayer with violent images of guns, criticizing the use of force or coercion in religious activities.

title: New Text

description.panel1: A character excitedly announces receiving a new text message.
description.panel2: Another character advises "Do not respond right away. You will look desperate.".
description.panel3: The first character explains "It is my dad, dude.".
description.panel4: The first character's dad anxiously awaits a response, thinking, "God he's desperate".

emotion: The author conveys that sometimes the advice to play it cool with texting doesn't apply, especially in personal relationships like family, where immediate responses might be more eagerly expected.

title: Fiddler Crab.

description.panel1: The crab introduces itself, claiming expertise in fiddles.
description.panel2: A fish corrects the crab, saying it is holding a viola.
description.panel3: The crab looks at the instrument, appearing confused.
description.panel4: The crab acknowledges its mistake with a resigned comment.

emotion: The author expresses humor and makes the reader smile by depicting the crab's confident but incorrect assumptions and its subsequent realization and admission of its mistake.

title: Mind Body Problem

description.panel1: A person demands their brain to provide good thoughts.
description.panel2: The brain, in turn, demands good sleep from the person.
description.panel3: The person angrily insists on receiving good thoughts first and flips a table.
description.panel4: The person yells at the brain for good sleep, while the brain defiantly refuses.

emotion: The author highlights the interdependent relationship and the often conflicting demands between mental well-being (good thoughts) and physical rest (good sleep), illustrating the frustration and difficulty in balancing the two.

title: Banana

description.panel1: A doctor mentions that "It is an operation so difficult, only two people in the world can do this.".
description.panel2: The doctor then says "One of them is dead".
description.panel3: The doctor urgently calls "Dr. Banana we need you in the emergency room.".
description.panel4: A banana is shown with a stethoscope around it.

emotion: The author uses absurd and unexpected ways to create humor that makes people smile.

title: Birthday Speech

description.panel1: A raccoon questions the idea of a higher purpose or reason for existence.
description.panel2: The same raccoon discusses the biological perspective of existence based on reproduction and survival.
description.panel3: The raccoon concludes that they have stopped searching for meaning and instead focus on enjoying life's pleasures.
description.panel4: The raccoon and its friends are seen celebrating a birthday with a cake and drinks.

emotion: The author expresses his irony about those inappropriate speeches by describing the raccoon's birthday speech.

title: lol

description.panel1: A person hears a dumb joke and suppresses a laugh.
description.panel2: The person starts giggling and admits that the joke wasn't that funny.
description.panel3: The person continues laughing uncontrollably, saying "STOP."
description.panel4: The person is on the ground laughing, while bystanders stare and comment.

emotion: The author illustrates the sometimes uncontrollable and exaggerated reaction people can have to even the simplest and silliest jokes, highlighting the humor in the situation and the social awkwardness that can result.

title: Feeling's Mutual.

description.panel1: A yellow character angrily points and says "I HATE YOU" to a blue character.
description.panel2: The blue character looks at the yellow character and responds, "I HATE ME TOO."
description.panel3: The yellow character looks pleasantly surprised and says, "OH WOW! WE HAVE SO MUCH IN COMMON..."
description.panel4: The yellow character hugs the blue character tightly.

emotion: The author expresses how mutual negative feelings can unexpectedly create a bond between individuals, highlighting the irony and complexity of human relationships and emotions.

title: Weekday Forecast

description.panel1: A blue character wishes a pink character a great Tuesday, to which the pink character responds by also wishing a great Tuesday.
description.panel2: The pink character, now sweating, forewarns of an awful Wednesday.
description.panel3: The pink character states that Thursday will be average while both characters hold coffee cups.
description.panel4: The blue character says Friday's situation will be revealed later, leaving it ambiguous.

emotion: The author expresses the emotions of each day of the work week and expresses anxiety about work.

title: the Volume is the Message

description.panel1: A bird expresses a desire to be loud enough for everyone to hear.
description.panel2: Another bird asks why and what the first bird wants to say.
description.panel3: The first bird pauses, considering the question.
description.panel4: The first bird responds that the message is less important than the volume.

emotion: The author satirizes those who only pay attention to form and ignore content, and believes that content is more important than form.

title: unpacking

description.panel1: A girl notices a box and asks "What's in that box?".
description.panel2: The girl says that "Looks like some of my old stuff" and another person PAL look at her.
description.panel3: They see a sketchbook with an old drawing inside.
description.panel4: The girl holding the sketchbook asks PAL, "Do you think mistakes are flammable?", looking disappointed.

emotion: The author uses the characters in this cartoon to express his dissatisfaction with his early works and uses embarrassment to show his ambition.

title: Relaxing

description.panel1: A red bird excitedly exclaims "I think I am doing it!".
description.panel2: The bird stands up and states "I am relaxing".
description.panel3: The bird struggles to maintain the feeling of relaxation, with a determined look.
description.panel4: The bird appears stressed and overwhelmed, shouting "Mindfulness."

emotion: The author expresses how trying too hard to relax and focus often leads to people becoming more anxious.

title: pokemon blues

description.panel1: A boy and an older man talk about a long-standing rivalry between the boy and the man's grandson.
description.panel2: The older man forgets the grandson's name and the boy suggests the name "Blue."
description.panel3: The older man pretends he was just testing the boy and both laugh uncomfortably.
description.panel4: The grandson, who is not named Blue, stands outside looking sad while two shadowy figures in the background laugh at him.

emotion: The author highlights the humor and potential hurt feelings in personal relationships when someone's name is forgotten and misidentified.

title: Most Afraid

description.panel1: Two birds are having a conversation, with one asking the other, "What are you most afraid of?"
description.panel2: One bird expresses "WELL... THAT ONE DAY I'LL WAKE UP AND FIND MYSELF ENTIRELY UNINTERESTED INTHE WORLD AROUND ME...UNABLE TO CONJURE CURIOSITYOR THE UNFAMILIAR ; NO THRILIN FORMER DELIGHTS; NOAPPETITE FOR CHANGE".
description.panel3: The first bird asks the second bird what it is afraid of.
description.panel4: The second bird simply replies, "Centipedes!"

emotion: The author humorously shows his thoughts on life through the humorous contrast between the two birds' most feared things, while also delivering a sense of absurdity.

title: Hand Down Justice.

description.panel1: The judge angrily sentences the defendant for heinous crimes.
description.panel2: The judge says the sentence is "a slap on the wrist," and the defendant sighs in relief.
description.panel3: The judge calls for "the slapper" to enter the courtroom.
description.panel4: A muscular character enters and greets the nervous defendant, preparing to administer the slap.

emotion: The author uses humor to critique the concept of lenient punishments for serious crimes by introducing an exaggerated, unexpected twist.

title: Please Answer My Question

description.panel1: Two birds are sitting on a branch; one bird asks if a leaf was green before and the other bird replies that summer is almost over.
description.panel2: The first bird, baffled, asks, "What?" and the second bird responds, "About time to head south for winter."
description.panel3: The first bird looks contemplative with colorful leaves around.
description.panel4: The first bird, now seemingly resigned or confused, repeats, "What?"

emotion: By describing the conversation between two birds, the author shows that sometimes people's thoughts jump a lot, causing the person you are talking to to be unable to keep up with your thoughts, which will make people who have similar experiences in life smile knowingly.

title: Tomatoes

description.panel1: A bird enthusiastically states that corn is the best seed.
description.panel2: Another bird counters that corn is technically a grain.
description.panel3: The first bird responds by saying "Tomato tomahto."
description.panel4: The first bird repeats "Tomato tomato, tomahto tomahto," and then says "no."

emotion: By describing the conversation between two birds, the author satirizes those people in life who try to cover up their mistakes by making unreasonable demands.

title: Rules

description.panel1: A blue character tells a red character that "Sir, you can't loiter there.".
description.panel2: The red character angrily asks "Do I look like someone that follows the rules?".
description.panel3: The blue character calmly replies "yes."
description.panel4: The red character confidently responds, "You're god damn right."

emotion: Through the dialogue between these two characters, the author humorously satirizes people who do not follow the rules in life.

title: the Tree

description.panel1: A person is sitting on a bench reading a book under a tree.
description.panel2: A small object, likely an acorn, falls from the tree towards the person.
description.panel3: The person looks up towards the tree with an annoyed expression.
description.panel4: The tree appears to be thinking "Fuck you."

emotion: The author expresses his thoughts on the relationship between man and nature by humorously depicting a tree with human-like thoughts and emotions.

title: Confidence

description.panel1: A bird questions itself, asking "Am I beautiful?"
description.panel2: The bird, in close-up, reflects and says "Bah... I guess it's all relative."
description.panel3: The bird thinks further and says "Are there uglier birds? Sure."
description.panel4: The bird confidently declares "Are there prettier birds? No."

emotion: The author conveys a message about self-confidence and perception, illustrating how self-reflection can lead to a strong sense of self-worth.

title: Thought That Counts.

description.panel1: A frog sits next to a turtle, saying, "I haven't done much with my life."
description.panel2: The frog continues, "But I've THOUGHT about doing a lot of things!" while the turtle responds with "whoa."
description.panel3: The frog enthusiastically adds, "You wouldn't believe how many great things I think about doing!"
description.panel4: The turtle replies, "That's very impressive," and the frog responds, "thank you."

emotion: The author satirizes those who can only think but not put it into practice.

title: The Purpose of Life.

description.panel1: Two characters asks a green alien in a spaceship, "What is the purpose of life?"
description.panel2: The alien answers its own question, saying, "To build a big ass pyramid," while one of the characters responds, "We did that already," and the other character nods in agreement.
description.panel3: The alien reacts by saying, "Whelp..."
description.panel4: The alien zooms away in its spaceship, leaving the two characters standing together.

emotion: The author humorously explores the meaning of life.

title: Way to go

description.panel1: Two characters bid farewell with one saying, "See you later!" and the other replying, "Later!"
description.panel2: The characters realize they are walking in the same direction and find it awkward.
description.panel3: The characters awkwardly notice they are taking the same bus.
description.panel4: The characters realize they are entering the same house and find it even more awkward.

emotion: The author uses a humorous way to illustrate the social awkwardness that may arise when two people who have said goodbye to each other continue to meet unexpectedly.

title: Guardian Angel

description.panel1: A blue angel says "Hello, I am your guardian Angel" to a pink character who responds with "Really?"
description.panel2: The pink character immediately questions the guardian angel's whereabouts.
description.panel3: The pink character angrily accuses the guardian angel "I took out hung student loans and you did not stopping me?".
description.panel4: The pink character continues blaming the angel "I majored in art history and you just let it happen".

emotion: The author satirizes the tendency people sometimes have to feel frustrated about their past decisions and to want someone or something to be responsible for their personal choices.

title: Changing Lanes

description.panel1: The driver raccoon asks the passenger raccoon to hand them the turn signal from the glove box.
description.panel2: The passenger raccoon opens the glove box.
description.panel3: The passenger raccoon hands a turn signal lever to the driver raccoon, who says "Thanks."
description.panel4: The driver raccoon holds the turn signal lever which actually is a gun and announces, "I'm changing lanes!"

emotion: The author humorously satirizes traffic problems by describing the driver's lane change, and hopes to cause readers to think about creating a good traffic environment.

title: Sticks and Stones.

description.panel1: A pink character confidently states that "Sticks 'N' stones may break my bones, but words will never hurt me!"
description.panel2: A large rock with sticks falls and crashes dramatically.
description.panel3: A doctor informs the pink character that no bones were broken, but calls them a "super-human freak."
description.panel4: The pink character begins to cry after repeating the word "freak."

emotion: The author uses humorous contrast to demonstrate the power of language and reminds readers to be careful with their words in life.

title: haven't seen it

description.panel1: The character with glasses notices a group of people discussing something they haven't seen.
description.panel2: One of the group asks the people with glasses how many times she have seen it.
description.panel3: The character with glasses nervously admits that she haven’t seen it.
description.panel4: The group reacts loudly and dramatically to the admission.

emotion: The author illustrates the social pressure and surprise that can occur when someone admits they haven't experienced or seen something that the majority assumes to be common knowledge or experience.

title: Cute

description.panel1: Two owls are depicted, and one owl is asking the other, "Do you think I'm cute?"
description.panel2: The second owl is looking at the first owl without responding.
description.panel3: The first owl is still looking at the second owl, waiting for a response.
description.panel4: The second owl finally responds, saying, "You're cute in an intense way, haha."

emotion: The author humorously depicts an interaction about self-perception and how it may be perceived by others, highlighting the idea that "cuteness" can be subjective and viewed differently by different individuals.

title: A Series of Cliched Jokes

description.panel1: A man at a bar tells an older woman that life feels like a series of cliched jokes, and she agrees with him.
description.panel2: The man encounters two other men; one says "Ugh" in response to something off-panel.
description.panel3: In a different setting, a group of people expresses disdain or indifference by saying "PFFT."
description.panel4: The man is at a different bar table with an angel holding a toaster, and he yawns.

emotion: The author conveys the idea that everyday life can often feel predictable and filled with cliched or repetitive scenarios, leading to reactions of boredom and disinterest.

title: Heaven Forbid

description.panel1: God says, "If you like Earth, you'll love Heaven."
description.panel2: Another character responds by saying he will move to Heaven, and the god replies that he have to die first.
description.panel3: The second character reacts with frustration, asking why he have to die.
description.panel4: The god explains that he don't make the rules, and the second character angrily points out that he make everything.

emotion: The author uses humor to illustrate that even God has to follow rules, emphasizing that the rules in life are sacred and inviolable.

title: Good Vibes Only

description.panel1: A purple character stands alone with an expressionless face.
description.panel2: The purple character approaches a blue character sitting on a cushion and waving.
description.panel3: A sign appears above them that reads "good vibes only."
description.panel4: The purple character walks away from the blue character.

emotion: The author seems to express the idea that not everyone may be willing to engage with or accept positive vibes, illustrating that personal preferences or circumstances can lead people to walk away from positivity.

title: Daddy Issues

description.panel1: A servant presents a skull to their master who instructs to place it with the others.
description.panel2: The servant follows the instruction and puts the skull on a shelf filled with other skulls.
description.panel3: A skull on the shelf begins to speak, stating that the master collects them to fill a void in their loveless existence.
description.panel4: The frustrated master retorts that psychoanalysts are not allowed and then hears the skull add that the master also has daddy issues.

emotion: The author uses dark humor to comment on the emptiness and underlying personal issues that can drive obsessive behaviors, and the potential frustration with psychoanalytic insights.

title: Dilemma

description.panel1: The title of the comic "The Eternal Dilemma" is shown.
description.panel2: A man is touching his chin and contemplating whether to shave his facial hair.
description.panel3: The man continues to touch his chin and expresses uncertainty about shaving.
description.panel4: The man considers the alternative of staying a hermit forever instead of shaving.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight the common and seemingly trivial decision-making process that can feel overwhelming, illustrating how mundane choices can sometimes be seen as significant dilemmas.

title: Snapped

description.panel1: A green character asks, "How's it going?" and the pink character responds, "Fine," while holding a snack.
description.panel2: The pink character snaps a chip in half while looking tense.
description.panel3: The pink character begins to cry.
description.panel4: The green character asks, "Hanging on by a thread?" and the pink character nods while crying, as the green character pats their back.

emotion: By describing the behavior of two people, the author illustrates that people may sometimes experience big emotional fluctuations due to very small things.

title: Oh My Goodness

description.panel1: A pink character is hiding in a bush, thinking "ACTS OF KINDNESS ARE MY FETISH."
description.panel2: A red character returns a dropped $100 bill to a yellow character, saying, "PARDON ME, SIR, BUT YOU DROPPED THIS $100 BILL."
description.panel3: The yellow character thanks the red character, who responds with "ANY TIME!" while the pink character watches from the bush.
description.panel4: The pink character in the bush appears flustered and thinks "OH GOD."

emotion: The author humorously illustrates how someone might find joy or excessive excitement in witnessing acts of kindness.

title: Mr. Lovenstein Presents: No Context

description.panel1: A red character receives a message on their phone saying, "Tonight, we should..." with a picture of two people engaging in some activity.
description.panel2: The red character sees another message that says, "AND..." with a picture of a pink figure doing an ambiguous action.
description.panel3: The red character receives a third message that says, "ALSO..." with a picture of a yellow figure holding an axe.
description.panel4: The red character, holding an axe, looks at another blue character who is holding a box titled "NO CONTEXT," and the blue character says, "Ready to play no context?" followed by the red character replying, "...OH."

emotion: The author satirizes some of the junk information on the Internet and the people who want to learn these things.

title: Magic Wand

description.panel1: A group of witches are gathered around a campfire discussing abolishing the patriarchy.
description.panel2: One witch encourages the others to raise their wands.
description.panel3: The witches raise their wands, but one witch's wand appears to be a magic wand vibrator.
description.panel4: The witch holding the magic wand vibrator looks awkward.

emotion: The author uses humor to highlight themes of female solidarity and empowerment, possibly suggesting that non-traditional tools can be powerful in their own right.
